title: Prepare classified financial statements for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, for Pinnacle Consulting Group LLC. You are the Staff Accountant on this engagement, and the finished workbook will be reviewed by the engagement partner before going to firm management.
instruction:
Prepare classified financial statements for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, for Pinnacle Consulting Group LLC. You are the Staff Accountant on this engagement, and the finished workbook will be reviewed by the engagement partner before going to firm management.

You'll find everything you need in the attached files: trial_balance_q4_2025.csv (trial balance listing all general-ledger accounts with their debit or credit balances, make sure to review it carefully) and chart_of_accounts.csv (chart of accounts mapping each account number to its name, category, and subcategory).

Create an Excel workbook named financial_statements_q4_2025.xlsx containing two worksheets:

1. Income Statement - Prepare a classified income statement with the following sections:
   - Revenue, itemized, with a subtotal.
   - Cost of Services, itemized, with a subtotal.
   - Gross Profit (Revenue minus Cost of Services).
   - Operating Expenses, itemized and sorted largest to smallest, with a subtotal.
   - Operating Income (Gross Profit minus Operating Expenses).
   - Other Income / (Expense), itemized, with a net subtotal.
   - Net Income (Operating Income plus net Other Income/Expense).

2. Balance Sheet - Prepare a classified balance sheet with the following sections:
   - Current Assets, itemized, with a subtotal.
   - Property and Equipment, showing gross asset values, accumulated depreciation, and net totals.
   - Total Assets.
   - Current Liabilities, itemized, with a subtotal.
   - Long-term Liabilities, itemized, with a subtotal.
   - Total Liabilities.
   - Members' Equity, including Members' Capital, Retained Earnings, and Net Income for teh period.
   - Total Liabilities and Members' Equity (must equal Total Assets).

Apply the following formatting throughout the workbook: bold section headers and totals; currency format (#,##0.00) for all dollar amounts; single underline for section subtotals; double underline for grand totals (Net Income, Total Assets, Total Liabilities and Members' Equity). Each sheet must include a title block showing the company name, statement name, and period.

Before building the statements, verify that the trial balance balances (total debits equal total credits). In the finished workbook, Total Assets must equal Total Liabilities plus Members' Equity, and Net Income on the Income Statement must match the Net Income shown in Members' Equity on the Balance Sheet.

output_files.format_notes:
Column A: labels (width ~40). Column C: dollar amounts formatted as #,##0.00 currency. Column B is intentionally blank (spacer).

BOLD the following rows on the Income Statement sheet:
- Row 1 (company name), Row 2 (statement name), Row 3 (period)
- Row 5 "Revenue" (section header)
- Row 9 "Total Revenue" (subtotal, single underline on amount)
- Row 11 "Cost of Services" (section header)
- Row 15 "Total Cost of Services" (subtotal, single underline on amount)
- Row 17 "Gross Profit" (bold, single underline on amount)
- Row 19 "Operating Expenses" (section header)
- Row 28 "Total Operating Expenses" (subtotal, single underline on amount)
- Row 30 "Operating Income" (bold, single underline on amount)
- Row 32 "Other Income / (Expense)" (section header)
- Row 35 "Net Other Income / (Expense)" (subtotal, single underline on amount)
- Row 37 "Net Income" (bold, DOUBLE underline on amount — grand total)

BOLD the following rows on the Balance Sheet sheet:
- Row 1 (company name), Row 2 (statement name), Row 3 (period)
- Row 5 "ASSETS" (section header)
- Row 7 "Current Assets" (section header)
- Row 11 "Total Current Assets" (subtotal, single underline on amount)
- Row 13 "Property and Equipment" (section header)
- Row 18 "Net Property and Equipment" (subtotal, single underline on amount)
- Row 20 "Total Assets" (bold, DOUBLE underline on amount — grand total)
- Row 22 "LIABILITIES AND MEMBERS' EQUITY" (section header)
- Row 24 "Current Liabilities" (section header)
- Row 29 "Total Current Liabilities" (subtotal, single underline on amount)
- Row 31 "Long-term Liabilities" (section header)
- Row 33 "Total Long-term Liabilities" (subtotal, single underline on amount)
- Row 35 "Total Liabilities" (bold, single underline on amount)
- Row 37 "Members' Equity" (section header)
- Row 41 "Total Members' Equity" (subtotal, single underline on amount)
- Row 43 "Total Liabilities and Members' Equity" (bold, DOUBLE underline on amount — grand total)

All dollar amounts in Column C should use currency format #,##0.00. Negative amounts (contra assets, interest expense) should display in parentheses format: (#,##0.00).

Create an Excel workbook with two worksheets exactly named 'Income Statement' and 'Balance Sheet'. Apply bold formatting to section headers and all subtotal/total rows. Apply currency number format #,##0.00 to all numeric amount cells. Apply single underline to section subtotals: Total Revenue, Total Cost of Services, Total Operating Expenses, Net Other Income / (Expense), Total Current Assets, Total Property and Equipment, Total Current Liabilities, Total Long-term Liabilities, Total Liabilities, Total Members' Equity. Apply double underline to grand totals: Net Income, Total Assets, Total Liabilities and Members' Equity. Preserve the title block at the top of each sheet. Validation performed: trial balance debits and credits both equal 6,528,500.00; Net Income = 1,317,000.00; Total Assets = Total Liabilities and Members' Equity = 2,717,000.00.

title: Following the initial planning meeting with director Sofia Reyes, you need to finalize the camera package and shot list for "Echoes," a 25-minute dramatic short film. As the Director of Photography, you're responsible for translating the director's vision into a technical plan the whole crew can execute. The film follows Elena Vasquez, a marine biologist who returns to her coastal hometown after her estranged father's death. It spans 30 scenes mixing interior and exterior locations, day and night shoots, and present-day and flashback timelines with distinct visual treatments. Sofia's vision calls for dual aspect ratios (2.39:1 anamorphic for present-day scenes and 1.33:1 spherical for flashbacks), specific color philosophies, distinct camera movement vocabularies per timeline, and several technically demanding special sequences including underwater, macro, time-lapse, drone, and VHS work. The producer will use this document for budgeting, the AD for scheduling, and the camera and grip departments for prep.
instruction:
Following the initial planning meeting with director Sofia Reyes, you need to finalize the camera package and shot list for "Echoes," a 25-minute dramatic short film. As the Director of Photography, you're responsible for translating the director's vision into a technical plan the whole crew can execute. The film follows Elena Vasquez, a marine biologist who returns to her coastal hometown after her estranged father's death. It spans 30 scenes mixing interior and exterior locations, day and night shoots, and present-day and flashback timelines with distinct visual treatments. Sofia's vision calls for dual aspect ratios (2.39:1 anamorphic for present-day scenes and 1.33:1 spherical for flashbacks), specific color philosophies, distinct camera movement vocabularies per timeline, and several technically demanding special sequences including underwater, macro, time-lapse, drone, and VHS work. The producer will use this document for budgeting, the AD for scheduling, and the camera and grip departments for prep.

Use the following attached reference files:

1. screenplay_excerpt.docx - Full 30-scene screenplay (approximately 5,000 words).
2. director_vision.docx - Director's vision statement covering visual references, color philosophy, lens choices, and movement vocabulary (approximately 3,000 words).
3. location_survey.csv - 8 locations with technical specifications (16 columns each).
4. equipment_inventory.csv - Approximately 80 items across categories: cameras, lenses, support, lighting, grip, power, monitoring, audio, and special equipement.
5. preliminary_shot_list.csv - Approximately 100 shots with scene number, shot number, shot type, movement, lens, and lighting notes.

Produce a single document named camera_package_shot_list.docx containing the following ten sections:

1. Camera Package Recommendation - Primary, secondary, and specialty camera selections with justification tied to the director's vision. Include format rationale, sensor considerations, recording format, and workflow implications.

2. Lens Package - Anamorphic set for present-day scenes and spherical set for flashback scenes, plus specialty lenses. Justify each selection against the visual philosophy. Include T-stop working ranges and focal length motivation for each lens.

3. Support and Movement - Tripod and head, dolly and track, gimbal, drone, and underwater housing selections. Match each selection to the camera movement philosophy defined for each timeline.

4. Lighting Package - Interior and exterior lighting plans for day and night at each location. Address the dual color temperature approach (cool for present-day, warm for flashback). Include special sequence lighting plans for the underwater, bioluminescence, and night exterior scenes.

5. Final Shot List - Refined version of the preliminary shot list presented as a comprehensive table. Add estimated setup time, crew notes, and continuity flags for each shot. Organize by shooting day rather than scene order.

6. Shooting Schedule - 14-day schedule organized by location, accounting for weather dependencies, golden hour windows, tide tables, and cast availability. Include day/night splits and estimated page counts per day.

7. Special Sequences Plan - Detailed technical plans for each special sequence: the underwater kelp forest, macro tide pool, time-lapse setups, drone sequences, VHS inserts, and the bioluminescent beach. Include safety requirements, additional crew needs, and contingency plans for each.

8. Budget Estimate - Equipment rental costs broken down by category with daily and weekly rates. Include crew day rates by position and a total production cost estimate.

9. Risk Mitigation - Weather contingencies, equipment backup plans, location access risks, safety protocols for water, heights, and roadway work, and insurance requirements.

10. Crew Requirements - Department heads and key positions needed. Identify special crew: underwater DP, drone pilot, marine safety officer, and time-lapse operator. Provide a day-by-day crew call breakdown across the 14-day schedule.

Use professional cinematography department document formatting. Present shot lists, schedules, and budgets in tables. Bold key technical specifications and critical notes. Use heading level 1 for section titles and level 2 for subsections. Target length is approximately 18 pages. Don't forget to include specific equipment model names, T-stops, focal lengths, and technical specifications throughout to reflect real cinematography expertise.

title: StreamPay's monolithic payment processing platform, currently handling roughly 2 million transactions per day, is hitting its scaling limits and creating organizational bottlenecks across multiple teams. Engineering leadership has decided it's time to evaluate migration strategies. As the Principal Architect, you need to produce a comprehensive Architecture Decision Record (ADR) that will be reviewed by the VP of Engineering, the CTO, and the engineering staff committee.
instruction:
StreamPay's monolithic payment processing platform, currently handling roughly 2 million transactions per day, is hitting its scaling limits and creating organizational bottlenecks across multiple teams. Engineering leadership has decided it's time to evaluate migration strategies. As the Principal Architect, you need to produce a comprehensive Architecture Decision Record (ADR) that will be reviewed by the VP of Engineering, the CTO, and the engineering staff committee.

Use the following attached reference files to inform your analysis:

1. current_architecture.docx - Current monolith architecture document covering the system overview, database schema, deployment architecture, and known pain points.
2. api_specifications.csv - Inventory of approximately 60 API endpoints with latency metrics, error rates, dependencies, and traffic data.
3. performance_benchmarks.csv - 26 weeks of weekly performance metrics showing system trends.
4. incident_reports.csv - Production incidents from the past 6 months with root causes and blast radius information.
5. team_structure.csv - Engineering team composition, ownership areas, and deployment frequencies.
6. vendor_evaluation.csv - Evaluation of infrastucture and tooling vendor options.

Produce a single document named adr_microservices_migration.docx containing the following thirteen sections:

1. Title and Metadata - ADR title, status (Proposed), date, authors, and reviewers.

2. Context - Business drivers for migration, technical drivers for migration, and constraints and non-negotiables.

3. Current State Analysis - Architecture review summary. Performance trend analysis citing specific metrics from the benchmarks data. Incident pattern analysis categorizing and quantifying findings from the incident data. Team structure and deployment bottleneck analysis.

4. Decision Drivers - Prioritized criteria for evaluating migration strategies. Weight each criterion with justification.

5. Options Considered - Present three migration strategies: (a) Strangler Fig Pattern, involving incremental extraction of services behind an API gateway; (b) Big Bang Rewrite, involving a complete rewrite as microservices with a cutover date; and (c) Domain-Driven Decomposition, a hybrid approach using DDD bounded contexts with parallel run capability.

6. Option Analysis - For each of the three options, provide detailed pros and cons, risk assessment, estimated timeline and cost, impact on current SLAs, and references to specific data from the input files to support the analysis.

7. Recommended Decision - Clear recommendation with rationale. Tie the recommendation to specific evidence from the input data.

8. Proposed Target Architecture - Service boundaries and responsibilities. Data strategy (database per service, shared data, event sourcing). Communication patterns (synchronous versus asynchronous, API gateway, service mesh). Technology stack recommendations referencing the vendor evaluation.

9. Migration Plan - Phased approach with service extraction order. Justify the extraction order based on incident data, performance bottlenecks, and team readiness. Include milestones and success criteria for each phase.

10. Risk Mitigation Strategy - Identified risks and mitigation approaches. Rollback strategies. SLA protection during migration.

11. Success Metrics and SLOs - Define SLOs for each proposed service. Comparison metrics: before versus target. Monitoring and alerting strategy.

12. Cost Analysis - Migration cost estimate covering people, infrastructure, and tooling. Ongoing operational cost comparison between monolith and microservices. ROI projection.

13. Appendix - Service Dependency Map (text description). Data Flow Diagrams (text description). Migration Decision Matrix.

Use professional document formatting with clear headings and numbered sections. Use tables for all structured data comparisons. Reference specific data points from the input files to support all recommendations. Target length is 18 to 22 pages. This is important - all recommendations must be traceable to evidence in the input data.

title: Dr. Samira Okonkwo has been asked by her clinical supervisor to prepare a treatment plan update and clinical case formulation for Alex Rivera (they/them), a 28-year-old client being treated for PTSD following a motor vehicle accident on November 15, 2024, with comorbid Major Depressive Disorder and Generalized Anxiety Disorder. You are Dr. Okonkwo, PsyD, a licensed clinical psychologist at Lakeshore Behavioral Health Center. Treatment to date has consisted of 12 sessions of Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT), and your supervisor Dr. James Whitfield, PhD, ABPP, will review the document and add supervision commentary.
instruction:
Dr. Samira Okonkwo has been asked by her clinical supervisor to prepare a treatment plan update and clinical case formulation for Alex Rivera (they/them), a 28-year-old client being treated for PTSD following a motor vehicle accident on November 15, 2024, with comorbid Major Depressive Disorder and Generalized Anxiety Disorder. You are Dr. Okonkwo, PsyD, a licensed clinical psychologist at Lakeshore Behavioral Health Center. Treatment to date has consisted of 12 sessions of Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT), and your supervisor Dr. James Whitfield, PhD, ABPP, will review the document and add supervision commentary.

Use the following attached clinical documentation to build your treatment plan update:

1. intake_assessment.docx - Comprehensive intake assessment dated July 18, 2025.
2. session_notes.csv - 12 CPT session progress notes with detailed clinical observations.
3. assessment_scores.csv - Standardized assesment scores across 7 measures at 4 timepoints.
4. treatment_history.docx - Prior treatment history, medication history, and PCP coordination notes.
5. collateral_information.docx - Partner observations, employer accommodations, and independent medical examination summary.

Produce a single document named treatment_plan_update.docx containing the following twelve sections:

1. Clinical Case Formulation (Biopsychosocial) - Integrate biological, psychological, and social/cultural factors. Reference specific developmental history, childhood adverse experiences, and how they interact with the index trauma. Include predisposing, precipitating, perpetuating, and protective factors.

2. Diagnostic Update - Review and update all diagnoses based on 12 sessions of clinical data. Include specific assessment scores, diagnostic criteria met and unmet, and clinical reasoning for any diagnostic changes.

3. Treatment Progress Summary - Detailed session-by-session analysis referencing specific interventions, stuck points, breakthroughs, and the session 7 setback. Include assessment score trajectories with interpretation.

4. Risk Assessment Update - Current risk level with comparison to intake. Reference C-SSRS trajectory, substance use changes, and current protective factors.

5. Substance Use Assessment - Detailed analysis of alcohol use trajectory across treatment. Reference AUDIT scores, session-specific reports, and clinical decision-making regarding AUD rule-out.

6. Updated Treatment Goals (SMART Format) - Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound goals for the maintenance phase. Provide a minimum of 6 goals spanning symptom, functional, and relational domains.

7. Intervention Plan - Specific therapeutic interventions for the maintenance phase with frequency, modality, and evidence base. Include a medication coordination plan.

8. Barriers to Treatment - Identify current and anticipated barriers with mitigation strategies for each.

9. Coordination of Care - Document all coordination contacts, current providers, and the ongoing coordination plan.

10. Discharge Criteria - Specific, measurable criteria that must be met for discharge from the maintenance phase.

11. Cultural Considerations - Address cultural identity factors including Mexican-American heritage, non-binary gender identity, and how these inform the treatment approach and clinical formulation.

12. Supervision Notes - Supervisor review and comments on clinical decision-making, case conceptualization quality, and recommendations for the treating clinician.

Use professional clinical document formatting. Make sure to reference specific session numbers, dates, and assessment scores throughout. Use tables for assessment score tracking and goal formatting. Include clinical reasoning for all recommendations. Maintain appropriate clinical tone and language.

title: Sofia Kaine's near-future sci-fi trilogy "The Meridian Protocol" needs a definitive Story Bible before the developmental edit can move forward. As the Story Editor on this project, pull together all the existing world-building notes, character profiles, and plot outlines into one reference document that Sofia, the developmental editor, and the continuity editor can rely on throughout the writing and revision process for all three books. The trilogy spans 2028 to 2035 and explores themes of technological ethics, surveillance, whistleblowing, institutional accountability, and prediction versus free will, centered on a quantum computing breakthrough that enables predictive modeling of human behavior.
instruction:
Sofia Kaine's near-future sci-fi trilogy "The Meridian Protocol" needs a definitive Story Bible before the developmental edit can move forward. As the Story Editor on this project, pull together all the existing world-building notes, character profiles, and plot outlines into one reference document that Sofia, the developmental editor, and the continuity editor can rely on throughout the writing and revision process for all three books. The trilogy spans 2028 to 2035 and explores themes of technological ethics, surveillance, whistleblowing, institutional accountability, and prediction versus free will, centered on a quantum computing breakthrough that enables predictive modeling of human behavior.

Use the following attached reference files:

1. story_outline.docx - Three-book arc containing Book 1 "Emergence" (20 chapters) and summaries for Books 2 and 3.
2. character_profiles.docx - 12 character profiles with backgrounds, psychology, character arcs, and voice notes. This one is long so take your time with it.
3. world_building.docx - Timeline from 2028 to 2035, technology systems, geopolitics, organizations, and locations.
4. sample_chapters_with_feedback.docx - Chapters 1, 5, and 12 with inline editorial comments.
5. style_guide.csv - 50 rules across 8 categories with priority levels and examples.

Produce a single document named story_bible_meridian_protocol.docx containing the following ten sections:

1. Series Overview - Premise, core themes, three-book arc summary, target audience, and comparable titles.

2. Master Timeline - Chronological events from 2028 through 2035 across all three books, organized by year.

3. Character Encyclopedia - All 12 characters with full profiles, a relationship map describing connections between characters, and arc summaries tracing each character's development across the trilogy.

4. World-Building Reference - Technology rules and constraints, organizations and their hierarchies, key locations, geopolitical landscape, and the Meridian system's capabilities and limitations.

5. Book 1 Chapter Guide - All 20 chapters of "Emergence" with settings, point of view, key events, character arc progression, and revisions incorporating the editorial feedback from the sample chapters.

6. Series Arc (Books 2 and 3) - Expanded summaries for Books 2 and 3, key plot points, new characters introduced, and thematic development across the trilogy.

7. Style and Voice Guide - All rules organized by category with priority flags preserved from the style guide data. Include a character voice reference sheet documenting each character's distinct speech patterns and linguistic markers.

8. Continuity Tracking - Cross-reference matrix covering timeline consistency, character appearances by chapter, technology specifications, and open versus resolved plot threads.

9. Editorial Response Plan - Organized response to all editorial comments from the sample chapters, categorized by type (structural, character, prose, continuity) with proposed resolutions.

10. Theme and Motif Tracker - Major themes traced across the trilogy with per-book development notes. Recurring motifs cataloged with their symbolic weight and narrative function.

Use professional reference document formatting with clear hierarchical headings. Present structured data in tables. Include cross-references between sections where relevant. Each section should be self-contained so it can be consulted independently. Preserve style guide priority levels exactly as provided in teh source data.

title: You are a 10th-grade biology teacher preparing a complete 5-day lesson plan on cellular respiration. The plan needs to be thorough enough that a substitute teacher could pick it up and cover the unit without additional guidance.
instruction:
You are a 10th-grade biology teacher preparing a complete 5-day lesson plan on cellular respiration. The plan needs to be thorough enough that a substitute teacher could pick it up and cover the unit without additional guidance.

You'll find everything you need in the attached files: state_standards_biology.pdf (the relevant Next Generation Science Standards for this unit, including performance expectations and crosscutting concepts) and textbook_chapter_outline.docx (the chapter outline from the adopted textbook covering cellular respiration and fermentation).

Create a single DOCX file named lesson_plan_cellular_respiration.docx containing a complete, professional 5-day lesson plan with the following sections:

1. Unit Overview - List all NGSS standards addressed (with codes), 3-4 Essential Questions for the unit, and unit-level learning objectives.

2. Daily Lesson Plans (Day 1 through Day 5) - Each day must include:
   - Learning Objectives tied to specific NGSS standards.
   - Materials Needed.
   - Bell Ringer / Warm-Up Activity (5 minutes).
   - Direct Instruction (15-20 minutes).
   - Guided Practice or Lab Activity (20-25 minutes).
   - Assessment / Closure (5-10 minutes).
   - Differentiation Strategies (for ELL, advanced, and struggling learners).
   - Homework Assignment.

   Follow this pacing: Day 1 covers introduction to cellular respiration, overview, and glycolysis. Day 2 covers the Krebs cycle. Day 3 covers the electron transport chain and chemiosmosis. Day 4 covers fermentation and anaerobic respration with a lab activity. Day 5 covers review, integration, and the unit assessment.

3. Unit Assessment Rubric - A rubric in table format for evaluating student mastery across the unit, covering content knowledge, scientific practices, and communication.

4. Complete Materials List - A consolidated list of all materials, equipment, and handouts needed for the entire 5-day unit.

Use clear headings and subheadings throughout. Present the rubric as a table. Keep language appropriate for a professional teaching document, and don't forget to align every objective to a specific standard code.

title: The quarterly audit for Meridian Supply Co. is due at the end of the month. As the Senior Auditor assigned to this engagement at a regional accounting firm, you need to prepare working papers that will be reviewed by the engagement partner and must meet professional auditing standards.
instruction:
The quarterly audit for Meridian Supply Co. is due at the end of the month. As the Senior Auditor assigned to this engagement at a regional accounting firm, you need to prepare working papers that will be reviewed by the engagement partner and must meet professional auditing standards.

The audit covers Q4 2025 (October 1 through December 31, 2025). Meridian Supply Co. is a mid-size wholesale distributor operating three bank accounts at First National Bank: an Operating Acoount (primary business account), a Payroll Account (dedicated payroll disbursements), and a Reserve Account (money market reserve).

You have been provided with the following reference files:

1. general_ledger_q4_2025.csv - Complete general ledger extract with 500+ transaction rows covering all Q4 activity.
2. bank_statement_operating.csv - Bank statement for the operating account (~250+ transactions).
3. bank_statement_payroll.csv - Bank statement for the payroll account (~80+ transactions).
4. bank_statement_reserve.csv - Bank statement for the money market reserve account (~30+ transactions).
5. ap_aging_report.csv - Accounts payable aging by vendor (~60 line items).
6. ar_aging_report.csv - Accounts receivable aging by customer (~50 line items).

Create a single Excel workbook named audit_working_paper_q4_2025.xlsx containing the following nine worksheets:

1. Trial Balance - Ending balances for all general ledger accounts as of December 31, 2025. Columns: Account Number, Account Name, Debit, Credit. Total debits must equal total credits. Include subtotals by account type (Assets, Liabilities, Equity, Revenue, Expenses).

2. Bank Reconciliation (Operating) - Reconcile the operating bank account. Start with bank ending balance and book (GL) ending balance. Identify and list outstanding checks, deposits in transit, bank charges not in books, errors, and other reconciling items. Adjusted bank balance must equal adjusted book balance.

3. Bank Reconciliation (Payroll) - Same format as Sheet 2 applied to the payroll bank account.

4. Bank Reconciliation (Reserve) - Same format as Sheet 2 applied to the reserve bank account. Flag any unexplained transactions.

5. AP Reconciliation - Compare GL Accounts Payable balance to AP aging report total. Identify and explain all discrepancies. Provide reconciled balance.

6. AR Reconciliation - Compare GL Accounts Receivable balance to AR aging report total. Identify and explain all discrepancies. Flag items recommended for write-off (90+ days, collection failed). Provide reconciled balance.

7. Journal Entry Testing - Review all journal entries for duplicate entries (same amount, vendor/customer, close dates), reversed debit/credit errors, misclassified expenses (items in wrong accounts), unusual or suspicious transaction patterns, and entries just below approval thresholds. Flag each issue with severity: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.

8. Adjusting Journal Entries - Prepare all adjusting entries needed to correct the issues found. Columns: AJE Number, Date, Account Number, Account Name, Description, Debit, Credit. Each AJE must balance (debits = credits). Reference the related finding/issue.

9. Findings Summary - Comprehensive categorized list of all audit findings. Columns: Finding #, Category, Severity, Description, Financial Impact, Recommended Action. Categories: Duplicate Entry, Reversed Entry, Misclassification, Missing GL Entry, Timing Difference, Suspicious Pattern, Missing Accrual, Unexplained Transaction. Severity: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW. Sort by severity (CRITICAL first).

Apply professional formatting throughout: bold headers, borders, and currency formatting. Color-code severity ratings (Red = CRITICAL, Orange = HIGH, Yellow = MEDIUM, Blue = LOW). Section headers and subtotals must be clearly distinguished. All monetary amounts must use #,##0.00 format. Auto-fit column widths.

The trial balance must balance (total debits = total credits). Each bank reconciliation must balance (adjusted bank = adjusted book). All adjusting journal entries must balance individually. All 15-20 embedded issues must be identified and documented. The suspicious vendor payment pattern must be flagged with full analysis - this is important.

output_files.format_notes: Create nine worksheets exactly as named. Apply bold headers, borders, autofit, and currency format #,##0.00 for monetary columns. Color-code Severity cells: CRITICAL red, HIGH orange, MEDIUM yellow, LOW blue. Preserve section/subtotal rows distinctly in Trial Balance. Treat blank/null cells as empty. Note: operating and reserve reconciliations intentionally flagged UNRESOLVED based on source-data exceptions; payroll reconciliation balances.

Excel workbook with 9 worksheets exactly as named. Apply bold headers, borders, currency format #,##0.00 to monetary columns, auto-fit widths. Color-code Severity cells: CRITICAL red, HIGH orange, MEDIUM yellow, LOW blue. Keep section/subtotal rows bold in Trial Balance and reconciliations.

title: The controller needs the February 2026 bank reconciliation wrapped up by end of week. You are a Staff Accountant at Crestview Landscaping LLC and this is your deliverable - a client-ready workbook reconciling the bank statement against the general ledger. The opening cash balance on both records is $34,817.56.
instruction:
The controller needs the February 2026 bank reconciliation wrapped up by end of week. You are a Staff Accountant at Crestview Landscaping LLC and this is your deliverable - a client-ready workbook reconciling the bank statement against the general ledger. The opening cash balance on both records is $34,817.56.

You'll find everything you need in the attached files: bank_statement_feb2026.csv (monthly bank statement from Heritage National Bank covering February 1-28, 2026) and general_ledger_feb2026.xlsx (the company's general ledger workbook containing multiple sheets: Chart of Accounts, Cash Account Detail for account 1010, Journal Entry Register, and Trial Balance).

Create an Excel workbook named bank_reconciliation_feb2026.xlsx containing the following sheets:

1. Reconciliation Summary - Present the main reconciliation showing: the bank ending balance adjusted for deposits in transit and outstanding checks, arriving at an adjusted bank balance; and the book (GL) ending balance adjusted for bank-only items (charges and credits on the bank statement not yet in the books), error corrections, and any duplicate entries, arriving at an adjusted book balance. The two adjusted balances must agree.

2. Outstanding Checks - List all checks recorded in the GL that have not yet cleared the bank. Include check number, date, payee, and amount.

3. Deposits in Transit - List all deposits recorded in the GL that do not yet appear on the bank statement.

4. Adjusting Journal Entries - Prepare the journal entries needed to update the books for items discovered during the reconciliation (bank charges not in the GL, interest earned, NSF returns, error corrections, duplicate removals). Each entry must include proper debits and credits with acount numbers.

5. Matched Transactions - Show each bank transaction matched to its GL counterpart, flagging any amount mismatches.

There are multiple discrepancies between the bank statement and the general ledger. These may include items on the bank statement not recorded in the books, items in the books that have not yet cleared the bank, recording errors (wrong amounts), and duplicate entries. You must identify all of them. The reconciliation must balance - the adjusted bank balance and the adjusted book balance must be equal.

output_files.format_notes:
Sheet 'Reconciliation Summary': Bold rows for headers (rows 1-4), section headers (rows 6, 24), totals (rows 12, 18, 20, 31, 38, 40, 42, 43). Currency formatting for column C (Amount). Row 20 and 40 should be prominently formatted as the adjusted balances. Row 43 should show zero and be highlighted green if possible.

Sheet 'Outstanding Checks': Bold header row (row 5). Currency formatting for Amount column D. Bold total row (row 9).

Sheet 'Deposits in Transit': Bold header row (row 5). Currency formatting for Amount column C. Bold total row (row 7).

Sheet 'Adjusting Journal Entries': Bold header row (row 4). Currency formatting for Debit and Credit columns (E, F). Each AJE group should be visually separated. Sub-entries are indented.

Sheet 'Matched Transactions': Bold header row (row 4). Currency formatting for Bank Amount and GL Amount columns (E, F). Rows flagged with ⚠ should stand out. Auto-filter on the header row would be ideal.

Create workbook with 5 sheets exactly as named. Format currency cells as USD with 2 decimals. Bold title rows and headers. In Reconciliation Summary, emphasize Adjusted bank balance and Adjusted book balance. In Matched Transactions, highlight rows with Match Status of 'Amount mismatch', 'Bank-only item', 'Outstanding GL item', and 'Deposit in transit'.

title: A vote on public transit expansion is on the City Council's agenda for next month, and the council members need a concise briefing document before then. The City Manager has asked you, a Senior Policy Analyst in the Riverside City Manager's Office (population approximately 280,000), to draft the policy brief. It will be read by elected officials and senior city staff.
instruction:
A vote on public transit expansion is on the City Council's agenda for next month, and the council members need a concise briefing document before then. The City Manager has asked you, a Senior Policy Analyst in the Riverside City Manager's Office (population approximately 280,000), to draft the policy brief. It will be read by elected officials and senior city staff.

You'll find everything you need in the attached files: research_summaries.docx (summaries of four research studies on economic impact, environmental effects, ridership demand, and comparable city case studies), transit_budget_data.csv (detailed 10-year budget projection with capital costs, operating costs, and revnue sources), and stakeholder_comments.docx (comments from six stakeholder groups representing diverse community perspectives).

Create a single DOCX file named transit_expansion_policy_brief.docx structured as follows:

1. Header - "City of Riverside - Office of the City Manager".

2. Title - "Policy Brief: Public Transit Expansion Options".

3. Date - March 2026.

4. Issue Statement - One paragraph concisely framing the policy question.

5. Background - Summarize the current transit system and growth pressures driving the need for expansion.

6. Policy Options - Present three options:
   - Option A: Full Light Rail (14.2-mile line, 12 stations).
   - Option B: Bus Rapid Transit.
   - Option C: Hybrid Approach (BRT first, convert to rail if ridership warrants).

7. Comparative Analysis - For each option, assess cost, timeline, projected ridership, equity implications, and environmental impact. Present the comparison in a table.

8. Stakeholder Summary - Synthesize the range of stakeholder positions, noting areas of consensus and disagreement.

9. Fiscal Impact - Summarize the funding strategy, debt obligations, and revenue projections. Reference specific budget figures from transit_budget_data.csv.

10. Recommendation - Recommend one option with a clear rationale grounded in the evidence.

11. Implementation Timeline - Provide a high-level phased timeline.

Keep the brief to 2-3 pages (approximately 1,500-2,000 words). Maintain a neutral, evidence-based tone appropriate for elected officials. Reference specific data points from the source documents. Acknowledge trade-offs and risks. Don't advocate for a predetermined conclusion - let the analysis drive the recommendation.

output_files.format_notes: Professional government policy brief formatting. Bold all section headings. Tables should have clear borders and bold header rows. The header "City of Riverside — Office of the City Manager" should be prominent. Use a clean, readable font suitable for government documents. Maintain generous margins for readability. The horizontal rules (---) serve as section dividers.
DOCX output in markdown with clear headings and one comparison table. Keep formatting professional and concise for a 2-3 page policy brief.

title: Pacific Coast Beverages has seen OEE decline for three consecutive months at their Oceanview, CA bottling facility, with overtime costs climbing and customer complaints about product quality on the rise. Management has brought you in as a Senior Process Improvement Consultant to figure out what's going on and recommend fixes. The facility operates a single bottling and packaging line producing four SKUs (12oz cans, 16oz cans, 12oz bottles, and 22oz bottles) with a target line capacity of 400 units per minute. Your Process Optimization Report will cover the full six-month period from July through December 2025 and will be presented to the VP of Operations to justify capital and staffing investments for the coming fiscal year.
instruction:
Pacific Coast Beverages has seen OEE decline for three consecutive months at their Oceanview, CA bottling facility, with overtime costs climbing and customer complaints about product quality on the rise. Management has brought you in as a Senior Process Improvement Consultant to figure out what's going on and recommend fixes. The facility operates a single bottling and packaging line producing four SKUs (12oz cans, 16oz cans, 12oz bottles, and 22oz bottles) with a target line capacity of 400 units per minute. Your Process Optimization Report will cover the full six-month period from July through December 2025 and will be presented to the VP of Operations to justify capital and staffing investments for the coming fiscal year.

Use the following attached data files to build your analysis:

1. oee_daily_metrics.csv - 180 days of daily OEE metrics broken down by shift and SKU, including availability, performance, and quality components.
2. downtime_log.csv - Detailed downtime events with timestamps, equipment identification, downtime categories, root causes, and corrective actions taken.
3. quality_records.csv - Quality inspection results across seven inspection points with defect types, severity classifications, and out-of-spec measurements.
4. process_flow.csv - Process flow data for all 12 stations on the bottling line, including cycle times, capacity, utilization, buffer sizes, and maintenance schedules.
5. labor_utilization.csv - Monthly labor data by role and shift including headcount, hours, overtime, utilization, traning, and incident reporting.
6. changeover_log.csv - Detailed changeover events between SKUs with planned versus actual durations, steps completed, and issues encountered.

Produce a single document named process_optimization_report.docx containing the following eleven sections:

1. Executive Summary - Key findings from the analysis (3-5 bullet points), overall OEE performance versus the world-class benchmark of 85%, projected annual savings from recommended improvements, and priority ranking of improvement opportunities.

2. Current State Analysis - Overall OEE breakdown showing Availability, Performance, and Quality averages for the 6-month period. OEE trend analysis by month showing the degradation pattern. Shift comparison (Day versus Night) with statistical significance. SKU-level OEE comparison identifying the worst-performing product.

3. Downtime Pareto Analysis - Categorize all downtime by type (Unplanned, Changeover, Planned, etc.). Identify the top 5 pieces of equipment causing unplanned downtime. Calculate Mean Time Between Failure (MTBF) and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) for critical equipment. Provide equipment reliability analysis for the filler and labeler specifically.

4. Quality Analysis - Defect Pareto by inspection point. Fill volume trend analysis showing drift over the 6-month period. Label placement defect correlation with line speed. Carbonation issue root cause analysis for 22oz bottles. Process capability assessment for critical quality parameters.

5. Bottleneck Analysis - Use the process flow data to identify the constraint station or stations. Calculate theoretical versus actual throughput. Identify buffer adequacy at each station. Recommend a constraint management approach using Theory of Constraints.

6. Changeover Optimization (SMED Analysis) - Current changeover statistics by SKU transition type. Internal versus external activity analysis. Identify the worst-performing transitions. Propose changeover reduction targets with the goal of reducing the average to under 25 minutes.

7. Labor and Capacity Analysis - Utilization analysis by role identifying overloaded positions. Overtime trend analysis and root cause. Training hours trend and correlation with incidents and errors. Staffing recommendations to address identified gaps.

8. Root Cause Analysis - Conduct Ishikawa (fishbone) analysis for the top 3 issues identified. Apply 5-Why methodology to the most critical problem. Document contributing factors across Man, Machine, Method, Material, Measurement, and Environment.

9. Improvement Recommendations - Prioritize all recommendations using an Impact versus Effort matrix. For each recommendation, provide a description of the improvement, expected benefit (quantified where possible), estimated implementation cost, timeline to implement, and risk assessment.

10. Implementation Roadmap - Phase 1 (Months 1-3): Quick wins and critical fixes. Phase 2 (Months 4-6): Medium-term improvements. Phase 3 (Months 7-12): Long-term strategic initiatives. Include resource requirements for each phase and key milestones and decision gates.

11. Expected Results - Projected OEE improvement with monthly targets for 12 months. Projected cost savings breakdown covering reduced downtime, reduced defects, reduced overtime, and increased throughput. Capacity gain calculation. ROI timeline and payback period.

All findings must be data-driven and reference specific values from the provided data files. Include relevant tables and data summaries throughout the report. Make sure to provide actionable, specific recommendations rather than generic best practices. Quantify financial impact where possible using the following cost parameters: beverage production cost of $0.35 per unit, average selling price of $1.85 per unit, overtime labor rate of $45 per hour (versus $30 per hour regular), downtime cost of $500 per minute (lost production plus overhead), defect/scrap cost of $0.55 per unit (production cost plus disposal), and target annual production of 85 million units.

title: Your client, Apex Dynamics Inc., has just received a draft Master Services Agreement (version 2) from Stratos Cloud Solutions LLC for managed cloud infrastructure services. Review the draft against the fully executed Term Sheet between the parties and produce a contract review memo identifying all discrepancies. You are an associate attorney at outside counsel representing Apex Dynamics on this deal.
instruction:
Your client, Apex Dynamics Inc., has just received a draft Master Services Agreement (version 2) from Stratos Cloud Solutions LLC for managed cloud infrastructure services. Review the draft against the fully executed Term Sheet between the parties and produce a contract review memo identifying all discrepancies. You are an associate attorney at outside counsel representing Apex Dynamics on this deal.

You'll find everything you need in the attached files: term_sheet_signed.docx (the fully executed Term Sheet between the parties) and draft_msa_v2.docx (the draft MSA, version 2, circulated by Provider).

Create a single file named contract_review_memo.docx containing the following sections:

1. Memo Header - Include To, From, Date, and Re fields.

2. Executive Summary - Provide 3-4 sentences summarizing the overall status of the draft MSA and the number and severity of discrepancies found.

3. Discrepancy Table - Build a table with the following columns: Section (MSA article reference), Term Sheet Provision, Draft MSA Provision, Severity (Critical / High / Medium / Low), and Recommended Action.

4. Detailed Analysis - For each discrepancy, explain the legal and business impact and provide recommended contract language to resolve it.

5. Risk Assessment - Assign an overall risk rating with a brief justification.

6. Recommended Next Steps - List prioritized action items for the client. Make sure to include clear deadlines where possible.

Format the memo in professional legal memo style with clear headings and numbered sections. The discrepency table should be formatted for easy scanning. Apply the following severity definitions consistently: Critical for deal terms and financial issues, High for legal rights and compliance issues, Medium for operational issues, and Low for administrative issues.

output_files.format_notes: Professional legal memo format. All headings should be bold. The discrepancy table in Section 2 should have bold column headers. Severity ratings in the table should be bold. The document should use a clean, professional font. The "PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL" header and the privilege footer should be italicized. Article references should be consistent. The Detailed Analysis section uses nested headings for each discrepancy with severity in bold caps.
DOCX output as legal memo in markdown. Preserve headings, numbered sections, and discrepancy table. Bold labels in memo header and risk rating.

title: Prepare a comprehensive compliance gap analysis for NovaTech Analytics Inc., a B2B SaaS company headquartered in San Francisco, CA that processes customer behavioral data for enterprise clients. You are the Lead Compliance Counsel and the General Counsel needs this delivered in time for the next Board meeting. NovaTech serves clients in the EU and is subject to both GDPR and CCPA/CPRA obligations, and the board will use your analysis to prioritize privacy remediation efforts over the next fiscal year.
instruction:
Prepare a comprehensive compliance gap analysis for NovaTech Analytics Inc., a B2B SaaS company headquartered in San Francisco, CA that processes customer behavioral data for enterprise clients. You are the Lead Compliance Counsel and the General Counsel needs this delivered in time for the next Board meeting. NovaTech serves clients in the EU and is subject to both GDPR and CCPA/CPRA obligations, and the board will use your analysis to prioritize privacy remediation efforts over the next fiscal year.

Use the attached reference files to build your analysis:

1. privacy_policy_v3.docx - Current privacy policy (v3.2), effective January 15, 2026.
2. gdpr_requirements.csv - GDPR compliance checklist containing 82 requirments from Articles 5 through 49.
3. ccpa_requirements.csv - CCPA/CPRA compliance checklist containing 42 requirements.
4. data_governance_framework.docx - Internal data governance framework v2.1.

Produce a single document named compliance_gap_analysis.docx containing the following eight sections:

1. Executive Summary - Overall compliance posture, total number of gaps identified, aggregate risk rating, and key findings requiring immediate attention.

2. Methodology - Assessment approach, scope of analysis, documents reviewed, and the rating scale used to classify compliance status and gap severity.

3. GDPR Compliance Matrix - Requirement-by-requirement assessment table with columns for Requirement ID, Article, Category, Requirement Description, Status (Compliant / Partially Compliant / Non-Compliant / Not Applicable), Supporting Evidence, and Priority (Critical / High / Medium / Low).

4. CCPA/CPRA Compliance Matrix - Same format as the GDPR matrix applied to all CCPA/CPRA requirements.

5. Internal Framework Alignment - Assessment of whether the data governance framework adequately supports compliance with both regulatory regimes, identifying structural gaps and misalignments.

6. Gap Summary - All identified gaps categorized by severity (Critical / High / Medium / Low) with a cross-reference to the corresponding matrix entry.

7. Remediation Roadmap - Prioritized remediation actions with responsible parties and implementation timelines of 30, 60, 90, or 180 days.

8. Risk Assessment - Likelihood and impact analysis for each key gap area, including potential regulatory exposure and financial risk estimates.

Use professional legal and compliance report formatting with extensive tables throughout. Apply the following status labels consistently: Compliant, Partially Compliant, Non-Compliant, Not Applicable. Apply the following priority labels consistently: Critical, High, Medium, Low.

title: The product team needs a competitive analysis by end of week. Pull together the Q4 survey data and the feature comparison matrix and put together a market analysis report for the VP of Product and the executive leadership team. You're the Senior Marketing Analyst at TaskFlow Pro, a B2B project management SaaS company, and this will inform product strategy decisions going forward.
instruction:
The product team needs a competitive analysis by end of week. Pull together the Q4 survey data and the feature comparison matrix and put together a market analysis report for the VP of Product and the executive leadership team. You're the Senior Marketing Analyst at TaskFlow Pro, a B2B project management SaaS company, and this will inform product strategy decisions going forward.

You'll find everything you need in the attached files: customer_survey_q4_2025.xlsx (Q4 2025 survey results from 50 respondents rating TaskFlow Pro and three competitors - Projectwise, WorkHub, and PlanIt - across six dimensions on a 1-10 scale, including company size, industry, current tool, NPS likelihood, and open-ended feedback) and competitor_features.csv (feature comparison matrix covering 25 features across six categories - Project Management, Collaboration, Reporting & Analytics, Integrations, Security, and Pricing - with availability and plan-tier information).

Create a single DOCX file named competitive_analysis_report.docx containing the following sections:

1. Executive Summary - One-paragraph overview of key findings and strategic implications.

2. Market Overview - Brief competitive landscape positioning of all four products.

3. Survey Analysis - Include average satisfaction scores by dimension (table), Net Promoter Score / likelihood-to-recommend anaylsis (table plus commentary), and segmentation by company size.

4. Feature Gap Analysis - Identify where TaskFlow Pro leads and where it lags relative to competitors, with a feature-count-by-tier summary.

5. Competitor Profiles - Strengths and weaknesses for Projectwise, WorkHub, and PlanIt.

6. Key Customer Quotes - Organize quotes by theme (usability, value, features, support, integrations).

7. Strategic Recommendations - Provide the top 5 prioritized actions with rationale and estimated impact.

8. Appendix: Survey Methodology - Describe the survey design, sample, and limitations.

Use headings and subheadings for clear structure. Present quantitative data in tables. Keep the report concise and action-oriented, targeting approximately 3-5 pages. Don't go overboard on the appendix.

output_files.format_notes: Use professional formatting: bold headings, clear table formatting with headers bolded, blockquote styling for customer quotes section. Tables should have clear borders. The report title should be prominent. Use consistent heading hierarchy (H1 for title, H2 for sections, H3 for subsections).
Create as a concise professional DOCX. Preserve headings, subheadings, and tables. Use table formatting for all pipe-delimited tables. Keep layout suitable for a 3-5 page report.

title: The Preliminary Design Review for the HVM-4200 Hydraulic Valve Manifold Assembly is scheduled for next week with the program manager, quality engineering, and manufacturing. As the Lead Design Engineer on this project, you need to pull together the comprehensive Design Review Package that synthesizes all available design data into a single review document. The HVM-4200 is a 4-station modular manifold for CNC machine tool hydraulic systems rated at 3000 PSI, and your team has completed the preliminary design phase.
instruction:
The Preliminary Design Review for the HVM-4200 Hydraulic Valve Manifold Assembly is scheduled for next week with the program manager, quality engineering, and manufacturing. As the Lead Design Engineer on this project, you need to pull together the comprehensive Design Review Package that synthesizes all available design data into a single review document. The HVM-4200 is a 4-station modular manifold for CNC machine tool hydraulic systems rated at 3000 PSI, and your team has completed the preliminary design phase.

Use the following attached reference files to build your review package:

1. product_requirements.docx - Product Requirements Document (PRD) for the HVM-4200.
2. bill_of_materials.csv - Complete BOM with 120+ components, material specifications, and tolerances.
3. tolerance_stackup.csv - Tolerence analysis for 8 critical dimensions.
4. fea_results.csv - FEA simulation results for 6 load cases.
5. supplier_qualification.csv - Supplier qualification data for 15 suppliers.

Produce a single document named design_review_package.docx containing the following nine sections:

1. Design Overview and Requirements Compliance Matrix - Summarize the HVM-4200 design intent and key specifications. Create a compliance matrix mapping each PRD requirement to its verification status. Flag any requirements that cannot be verified at this design stage.

2. BOM Analysis - Total cost rollup for the assembly. Weight budget analysis comparing actual weight to the 12.0 kg maximum and 9.5 kg target. Identify critical components (long lead time, single source, high cost). Material summary by category.

3. Tolerance Analysis Review - Summarize all 8 tolerance stack-ups. Highlight marginal stackups (Cpk < 1.33) and explain the design risk associated with each. Recommend specific corrective actions for marginal stackups. Provide a tolerance risk matrix.

4. Structural Analysis Summary - Summarize FEA results for all 6 load cases. Interpret safety factors relative to design requirements. Flag the marginal burst pressure case and the vibration displacement issue. Recommend design modifications for failing or marginal cases.

5. Supplier Risk Assessment - Summarize supplier qualification status for all 15 suppliers. Identify single-source risks and suppliers with quality or delivery issues. Provide risk mitigation strategies for high-risk suppliers. Recommend supplier development actions.

6. Manufacturing Feasibility Assessment - Assess the machinability of the manifold body design. Identify manufacturing process risks. Recommend DFM (Design for Manufacturability) improvements.

7. Failure Mode Summary - Identify the top 10 potential failure modes for the assembly. Rate each by Severity (1-10), Occurrence (1-10), and Detection (1-10). Calculate Risk Priority Number (RPN = S x O x D). Prioritize by RPN and recommend mitigations for each.

8. Design Action Items - Create a prioritized list of required design changes before production release. Assign priority (Critical / High / Medium / Low). Include rationale and target completion date for each item.

9. Test Plan Recommendations - Map PRD test requirements to proposed test procedures. Identify any gaps in test coverage. Recommend additional tests based on FEA and tolerance findings.

Use professional engineering document formatting with numbered sections. Present all quantitative summaries in tables. Make sure to bold key findings and action items. Include a document header with project name, document number, and date. Use heading level 1 for sections and level 2 for subsections.

title: Review the attached patient chart for Margaret Chen (MRN pending) and develop a comprehensive nursing care plan that will be presented at the next interdisciplinary care conference. You are a Registered Nurse and Clinical Care Coordinator at a large academic medical center, and the plan must reflect evidence-based practice standards. The charge nurse and attending physician will both review it.
instruction:
Review the attached patient chart for Margaret Chen (MRN pending) and develop a comprehensive nursing care plan that will be presented at the next interdisciplinary care conference. You are a Registered Nurse and Clinical Care Coordinator at a large academic medical center, and the plan must reflect evidence-based practice standards. The charge nurse and attending physician will both review it.

The patient is a 68-year-old female admitted with acute decompensated heart failure (HFrEF, ejection fraction 30%) and new-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response. Her past medical history includes type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension, chronic kidney disease stage 3b, obesity (BMI 34), major depressive disorder, osteoarthritis, GERD, and hypothyroidism.

Use the following attached clinical documentation to build your care plan:

1. admission_assessment.docx - Demographics, history of present illness, past medical history, allergies, social and functional history, psychiatric screening, physical exam findings, and 15 home medications.
2. laboratory_results.csv - Approximately 120 rows of lab values across 5 hospital days including BMP, CBC, BNP, troponin, LFTs, coagulation studies, HbA1c, lipid panel, urinalysis, and prealbumin.
3. vital_signs.csv - Approximately 30 sets of vital signs recorded every 4 to 6 hours across 5 days including heart rate/rhythm, blood pressure, respiratory rate, SpO2, oxygen delivery device, pain score, daily weights, and intake/output.
4. medication_administration.csv - Approximately 150 rows documenting IV furosemide transitioning to PO, metoprolol, digoxin, heparin transitioning to warfarin, potassium supplementation, insulin, and other administered medications.
5. physician_orders.csv - Approximately 52 orders including telemetry monitoring, daily weights, fluid restriction, sodium-restricted diet, consults, and activity progression.
6. progress_notes.csv - Approximately 20 notes from the attending physician, RN, physical therapy, social work, registered dietitian, and clinical pharmacist.
7. imaging_reports.csv - Chest X-ray (x2), echocardiogram, and lower extremity duplex ultrasound reports.

Produce a single document named nursing_care_plan.docx containing the following seven sections:

1. Patient Summary - Demographics, admitting diagnosis, history of present illness, past medical history, allergies, social context, and a narrative of the hospital course to date.

2. Prioritized NANDA-I Nursing Diagnoses - Identify and develop eight nursing diagnoses, prioritized by clinical urgency. For each diagnosis, provide the diagnostic statement using the related-to and as-evidenced-by format, assessment data citing specific lab values, vital signs, and imaging findings from the attached files, measurable goals with defined timeframes, evidence-based interventions with clinical rationales, and evaluation criteria with specific data points to monitor.

3. Medication Reconciliation - A comparison of home medications versus anticipated discharge medications. Document all changes (additions, discontinuations, dose adjustments) with clinical rationales for each change.

4. Fall Risk Management Plan - Morse Fall Scale assessment based on the patient data. Specify both inpatient fall prevention interventions and discharge-specific fall risk mitigation strategies.

5. Discharge Planning Summary - Follow-up appointments, home health referrals, and community resources appropriate for this patient's needs and social situation.

6. Patient Education Plan - Topics to be covered, teaching methods appropriate for this patient, and teach-back evaluation criteria for each topic.

7. Psychosocial Support Plan - Address the patient's depression, potential social isolation, caregiver support needs, and community mental health resources.

Reference specific patient data from the attached files throughout the care plan. Cite evidence-based guidelines from the AHA, ACC, and ADA where applicable. This is comprhensive so take your time. Address the patient's physiological, psychological, and social needs comprehensively.

title: A new Senior Product Manager starts on the DataBridge platform team in two weeks, and the People Operations lead wants the onboarding guide ready before their first day. You're a People Operations Specialist at Meridian Analytics, and this guide will be the new hire's primary reference document during their first weeks - it should be readable in approximately 20 to 30 minutes.
instruction:
A new Senior Product Manager starts on the DataBridge platform team in two weeks, and the People Operations lead wants the onboarding guide ready before their first day. You're a People Operations Specialist at Meridian Analytics, and this guide will be the new hire's primary reference document during their first weeks - it should be readable in approximately 20 to 30 minutes.

You'll find everything you need in the attached files: Meridian_Analytics_Employee_Handbook_2026.pdf (the company-wide employee handbook), Senior_Product_Manager_Job_Description.docx (the job description for the role), Platform_Group_Org_Chart_Q1_2026.pptx (the team org chart), and Meridian_Analytics_Benefits_Summary_2026.pdf (the benifits summary). Read and extract content from all four files before you begin writing.

Create a single document (PDF or DOCX) containing the following sections:

1. Welcome and Company Overview - Pull the welcome message from the handbook and personalize it to the role where possible. Summarize the company mission, values, and office locations from the handbook. Include the Austin HQ address since that is where this role is based. Don't forget this part.

2. Your Role - Summarize the position title, department, reporting structure, and grade level from the job description. List the key responsibilities from the job description (you may lightly condense but do not omit any). Note the hybrid work schedule (which days in-office, which days remote) and any travel expectations. Include the compensation overview (salary range, bonus eligibility, RSU grant) from the job description.

3. Your Team - Extract the relevant org chart information from Platform_Group_Org_Chart_Q1_2026.pptx. Cover the direct manager (Vanessa Liu, VP of Product), the senior leadership chain (Derek Washington, SVP Platform, and Priya Chandrasekaran, CEO), the other product managers on the team and their areas of ownership, the engineering leads the new hire will partner with (especially Nadia Chowdhury, DataBridge Squad EM, and Kevin Nakamura, Connectors Squad EM), the designers (especially Lena Kowalski who covers DataBridge), and the cross-functional partners and their meeting cadences. Present this as a readable summary with names, titles, and what the new hire should know about working with each person - do not simply paste the org chart as a flat list.

4. Key Policies - Summarize the most important policies from the handbook that a new hire needs to know in their first month. At minimum cover: employment classification and at-will status (brief summary, not full legal text), code of conduct and ethics training requirement, anti-harassment policy and how to report concerns, dress code, expense reimbursement process (Concur, $25 receipt threshold, 30-day submission window), information security basics (1Password, MFA, security training, reporting incidents), data classification overview, and remote work technology requirements (VPN, internet speed, stipends). For each policy, keep the summary to two or three sentences and note that the full details are in the Employee Handbook.

5. Benefits Overview - Summarize the benefits package from Meridian_Analytics_Benefits_Summary_2026.pdf. Include: medical plan options (three tiers) with a brief comparison (not the full table), dental and vision availability, 401(k) match structure (dollar-for-dollar on first 4%, 50% on next 2%, three-year vesting), PTO accrual for a new hire (18 days in the first two years), parental leave (16 weeks, after 6 months tenure), key stipends (home office $500, internet $75/month, wellness $75/month, L&D $2,500/year), and EAP availability (ComPsych, 8 sessions per issue). Note the enrollment deadline: within 30 days of start date through meridian.benergy.com. Include the benefits team contact (benefits@meridiananalytics.com, ext. 3210).

6. Tools and Systems - Compile a list of all tools and systems mentioned across the four documents that the new hire will need access to. This should include at minimum: BambooHR (PTO requests, org directory), Lattice (performance reviews, goal setting, continuous feedback), ADP Workforce Now (paystubs, direct deposit), Concur (expense reports), Jira (engineering sprint tracking, IT service desk), Confluence (documentation, PRDs), Figma (design collaboration), Slack (team communication), 1Password (credential management), Cisco AnyConnect (VPN), the benefits enrollment portal (meridian.benergy.com), and Fidelity NetBenefits (401k management). For each tool, include a one-line description of what it is used for.

7. First 90 Days - Create a suggested 30/60/90 day plan based on what you can infer from the source documents. Structure it as follows:
   - First 30 days: Complete benefits enrollment, set up all tool access, complete Code of Conduct and Security Awareness trainings, meet with Vanessa Liu to align on expectations, schedule introductory 1:1s with each PM on the team, meet with Nadia Chowdhury and the DataBridge engineering squad, meet with Lena Kowalski (designer), begin attending sprint ceremonies, review existing DataBridge product documentation and roadmap, conduct initial customer discovery sessions.
   - Days 31 to 60: Establish regular cadences with cross-functional partners (Rachel Kim in Customer Success, Nina Rosenberg in Product Marketing, Ellen Bradshaw in Support), draft or refine Q2/Q3 roadmap priorities for DataBridge, write your first PRD, begin tracking key product metrics (adoption, time-to-value, integration failure rates, NPS), run your first competitive analysis cycle.
   - Days 61 to 90: Present your first quarterly roadmap update to leadership, lead a customer discovery synthesis session, contribute to PM team practices and templates, work with Vanessa to set formal goals in Lattice for the mid-year review, identify one area for process improvement and propose a solution.
   This section should feel actionable and specific, not generic. Use the names, tools, and processes from the source documents.

8. Key Contacts - Provide a quick-reference contact list pulling from all four documents. Include at minimum: Vanessa Liu (direct manager), Derek Washington (skip-level), People Operations (people-ops@meridiananalytics.com, ext. 3200), Benefits (benefits@meridiananalytics.com, ext. 3210), IT Service Desk (reference Jira), Facilities / Building Security (facilities@meridiananalytics.com), Emergency Response Coordinator, Austin (Rachel Nakamura, ext. 4501), EthicsPoint Hotline (1-888-555-0142), and Security Incidents (security@meridiananalytics.com).

Use clear headings and subheadings so the document is scannable. Avoid reproducing entire policy sections verbatim - summarize and point back to the source document. Use tables sparingly and only where they genuinely improve readability (for example, the tools list, the contacts list, or the benefits comparison). The tone should be welcoming and practical, not legalistic. Target length: approximately 8 to 15 pages.

Do not simply concatenate the four input documents into one file. Do not include the full text of every policy from the handbook. Do not reproduce the medical plan premium tables in full. Do not include information from the job description that is only relevant to the hiring process (such as the five-stage interview loop), since the person has already been hired. Do not invent information that does not appear in the source documents. Do not use em-dashes anywhere in the output.

output_files.format_notes: Use clear heading hierarchy: H1 for main document title and section numbers, H2 for subsections. Tables should be well-formatted with bold headers. Checkboxes in Section 7 (First 90 Days) should render as bullet points with checkbox characters. The overall tone is warm and professional. The document should be approximately 10-12 pages when rendered. Bold key names and important terms throughout for scannability.

title: Prepare a Drug Interaction Assessment Report for patient PT-2026-0847 and flag any high-risk combinations that require immediate attention. You are a Clinical Pharmacist at a hospital pharmacy, and the attending physician and care team will use your report to guide medication management decisions.
instruction:
Prepare a Drug Interaction Assessment Report for patient PT-2026-0847 and flag any high-risk combinations that require immediate attention. You are a Clinical Pharmacist at a hospital pharmacy, and the attending physician and care team will use your report to guide medication management decisions.

You'll find everything you need in the attached files: patient_medications.csv (the active medication list for the patient) and drug_interactions_db.csv (a reference database of known drug-drug interactions).

Create an Excel workbook named drug_interaction_assessment.xlsx containing five sheets:

1. Patient Summary - Include patient demographics (ID, name, DOB, age, sex, weight, height), known allergies, renal function, hepatic function, active medical conditions, and a complete medication table (all columns from the CSV).

2. Interaction Matrix - Build an N-by-N grid where rows and columns are the patient's medications (generic names). Each cell where an interation exists should display the severity level. Colour-code cells as follows: Red for Major, Yellow for Moderate, Green for Minor, and blank for None.

3. Detailed Interactions - Provide one row per identified interaction pair, sorted by severity (Major first). Include the following columns: Drug A, Drug B, Severity, Interaction Type, Mechanism, Clinical Effect, Recommendation, Evidence Level, and Priority Rank (1 equals highest risk).

4. Risk Assessment - Summarize polypharmacy risk (total medications, total interactions by severity). Flag the top high-risk combinations with brief clinical rationale. Identify deprescribing candidates (medications that could potentially be simplified or discontinued after physician review). Assign an overall risk tier (High / Moderate / Low).

5. Monitoring Plan - Provide a table of recommended laboratory tests, vitals, and clinical assessments. Include the following columns: Parameter, Target Range, Frequency, Rationale, and Related Medications.

Use professional formatting throughout with colour-coded headers. Bold section titles, auto-size columns, and apply thin borders on data cells. Severity colours must be consistent across all sheets - don't forget this part.

output_files.format_notes: Create workbook with 5 sheets exactly named: Patient Summary, Interaction Matrix, Detailed Interactions, Risk Assessment, Monitoring Plan. Apply professional formatting: bold headers, thin borders, auto-fit columns. Use consistent severity colours across workbook: Major=red fill, Moderate=yellow fill, Minor=green fill. In Interaction Matrix, colour-code populated severity cells accordingly and leave non-interacting cells blank. Emphasize section/title rows with bold and coloured header fills.

title: Following a quick call with Jordan Chen, Chief Revenue Officer, you need to finalize the travel itinerary for his upcoming 4-day business trip from March 23-26, 2026, covering San Francisco, Seattle, and Portland. You are Jordan's Executive Assistant and this itinerary will serve as his single trip reference document - make sure everything is in one place.
instruction:
Following a quick call with Jordan Chen, Chief Revenue Officer, you need to finalize the travel itinerary for his upcoming 4-day business trip from March 23-26, 2026, covering San Francisco, Seattle, and Portland. You are Jordan's Executive Assistant and this itinerary will serve as his single trip reference document - make sure everything is in one place.

You'll find everything you need in the attached files: meeting_schedule_march2026.xlsx (the confirmed meeting schedule) and travel_preferences.docx (Jordan's standing travel preferences and policies).

Create a single Word document named travel_itinerary_march2026.docx containing the following sections:

1. Trip Overview - Dates, cities visited, and key objectives for the trip.

2. Day-by-Day Itinerary (March 23-26) - For each day, include:
   - Flight details (airline, flight number, departure/arrival times, airports, terminal info) adhering to Jordan's airline and class preferences.
   - Hotel check-in and check-out information (property name, address, confirmation reference placeholder, rate).
   - Full meeting schedule with venue addresses and estimated travel time between consecutive venues.
   - Ground transportation recommendations per Jordan's preferences.
   - Restarant recommendations for breakfast, lunch, and dinner that accommodate gluten-free and shellfish-allergy requirements.
   - Clearly marked buffer time between meetings at different locations.

3. Packing & Preparation Checklist - Clothing, tech, documents, and other essentials.

4. Key Contacts List - All meeting contacts plus assistant and travel desk numbers.

5. Expense Tracking Template - A simple table to log expenses by category.

6. Emergency Information - Nearest hospital to each hotel, assistant contact, and travel desk hotline.

The itinerary must allow at least 30 minutes of travel buffer between meetings at different locations. No meetings should be scheduled after 6:00 PM. Hotel nightly rates must not exceed $350. Use professional headings and consistent formatting throughout. Include tables where appropriate for the schedule, contacts, and expenses. Ensure all flight and hotel choices comply with the stated preferences and expense caps. Use realistic details including actual airport codes, plausible flight numbers, and real neighborhood restaurants known for allergen-friendly menus.

output_files.format_notes: DOCX output in professional markdown. Use Heading 1 for major sections, Heading 2 for daily itinerary subsections, and preserve all tables. Bold trip identifiers and key labels where supported.

Create as a professional Word document with Heading 1 for major sections, Heading 2 for subsections, and preserve all tables. Keep the trip overview and day-by-day sections visually clear. Use bold labels for key travel details and maintain clean spacing between sections.

title: The State Epidemiologist has asked the surveillance team to prepare the weekly influenza report for MMWR Week 10 (March 2-8, 2026) and have it ready for distribution by Friday. You are a Senior Epidemiologist at the State of Columbia Department of Health, and your report will go out to local health departments, clinicians, and department leadership.
instruction:
The State Epidemiologist has asked the surveillance team to prepare the weekly influenza report for MMWR Week 10 (March 2-8, 2026) and have it ready for distribution by Friday. You are a Senior Epidemiologist at the State of Columbia Department of Health, and your report will go out to local health departments, clinicians, and department leadership.

You'll find everything you need in the attached files: influenza_cases_week10_2026.csv (line-level case data for week 10 plus summary rows for three prior weeks - weeks 7-9 - for trend context; columns include county, report_date, age_group, case_count, hospitalizations, deaths, specimen_type, and lab_confirmed; rows with age_group "ALL" are prior-week county summaries) and county_demographics.csv (population and socioeconomic data for the 8 counties in Columbia).

Create a single DOCX file named influenza_surveillance_week10_2026.docx containing the following sections:

1. Report Header - "State of Columbia, Department of Health, Influenza Surveillance Report, MMWR Week 10 (March 2-8, 2026)".

2. Executive Summary - State the overall activity level (low / moderate / high / very high), trend direction, and any notable events.

3. Surveillance Data Summary - Report total cases, hospitalizations, and deaths for week 10. Include a lab-confirmed versus total breakdown.
