4. Geographic Distribution - Provide a table of cases and incidence rates (per 100,000) by county. Identify the top-2 hotspot counties.

5. Age Distribution Analysis - Provide a table of case counts and rates per 100,000 by age group. Identify the most-affected age group.

6. Strain Distribution - Break down cases by specimen type (A/H1N1, A/H3N2, B/Victoria). Discuss the dominant circulating strain.

7. Trend Analysis - Compare week 10 totals with weeks 7-9. State whether activity is increasing, stable, or decreasing. Include a summary table with columns for week, total cases, hospitalizations, and deaths.

8. Hospitalization & Mortality - Calculate the case-fatality rate (CFR) and hospitalization rate. Identify high-risk groups.

9. Reccomendations - Provide actionable public health guidance covering vaccination messaging, targeted outreach for high-risk groups, and healthcare-provider alerts.

10. Methodology Notes - Briefly describe data sources and rate-calculation methods.

Use tables for all quantitative summaries. Bold key findings within narrative paragraphs. Use heading level 1 for the report title and heading level 2 for section headings. Keep the report concise and suitable for distribution to local health departments and clinicians. Don't let it get too long.

output_files.format_notes: Use Heading 1 for the report title (first line). Use Heading 2 for all section headings (## level). Bold all key findings within narrative text as marked. Format all tables with clear borders and bold header rows. Use consistent professional formatting throughout suitable for distribution to local health departments and clinicians.
DOCX report in markdown with Heading 1 for title and Heading 2 for sections. Preserve tables as pipe-delimited markdown. Bold key findings within narrative paragraphs.

title: Cascadia's 2025-2026 influenza season has been unusually severe, peaking earlier than typical seasons, and county health officers are asking for a formal investigation report. You are the Lead Epidemiologist at the Cascadia State Department of Health. Your report will be distributed to county health officers, hospital administrators, and state officials to guide immediate response actions and resource allocation decisions.
instruction:
Cascadia's 2025-2026 influenza season has been unusually severe, peaking earlier than typical seasons, and county health officers are asking for a formal investigation report. You are the Lead Epidemiologist at the Cascadia State Department of Health. Your report will be distributed to county health officers, hospital administrators, and state officials to guide immediate response actions and resource allocation decisions.

The investigation covers Epidemiological Weeks 40 through 51 (October through December 2025) across 15 counties in the state of Cascadia. Use the following attached surveillance and reference data files to build your report:

1. case_surveillance_12weeks.csv - 12 weeks of influenza case data (Epi Weeks 40-51, October through December 2025). Columns: epi_week, county, age_group (0-4, 5-17, 18-49, 50-64, 65+), sex (M/F), case_count, hospitalizations, icu_admissions, deaths, specimen_type (A/H1N1, A/H3N2, B/Victoria, B/Yamagata), lab_confirmed (Y/N), outbreak_associated (Y/N). Data spans 15 counties with varying population sizes and demographics. Note that some county/week combinations may show anomalous patterns warranting further investigation.

2. laboratory_results.csv - Laboratory testing data by week and reference lab. Columns: epi_week, lab_name, specimens_tested, specimens_positive, pct_positive, subtype_h1n1, subtype_h3n2, subtype_b_victoria, subtype_b_yamagata, antiviral_resistance_tested, oseltamivir_resistant. Covers 5 reference laboratories across 12 weeks. Track subtype distribution changes over the season. This is important for the surveilance narrative.

3. hospitalization_data.csv - Aggregated hospitalization records by week and county. Columns: epi_week, county, age_group, admissions, avg_los_days, icu_pct, mechanical_ventilation_pct, antiviral_treated_pct, underlying_conditions_pct, discharged, in_hospital_deaths. Pay special attention to ICU capacity indicators in individual counties.

4. vaccination_coverage.csv - Vaccination rates by county and age group. Columns: county, age_group, population, doses_administered, coverage_pct, coverage_change_from_prior_year. Examine the correlation between vaccination coverage and case rates.

5. county_demographics.csv - County reference and infrastructure data. Columns: county, population, pop_density_per_sq_mi, median_age, pct_65_plus, pct_uninsured, hospital_beds, icu_beds, primary_care_per_100k. Use for rate calculations and healthcare capacity analysis.

Produce a single document named influenza_outbreak_investigation_2025.docx containing the following fourteen sections:

1. Executive Summary - Overall severity assessment of the 2025-2026 season, key findings (3-5 bullet points), critical action items requiring immediate attention, and a 1-2 paragraph narrative summary.

2. Background and Surveillance Methods - Description of Cascadia's influenza surveillance system, data sources and reporting mechanisms, case definitions used, time period and geographic scope of the investigation, and limitations of surveillance methodology.

3. Descriptive Epidemiology (Person, Place, Time) - Total case counts, hospitalizations, ICU admissions, and deaths for the 12-week period. Breakdown by sex. Overall incidence rate per 100,000 population. Case-fatality rate and hospitalization rate. Lab confirmation rate.

4. Epidemic Curve Analysis - Week-by-week case counts showing the epidemic curve. Identification of the peak week and comparison to typical season timing. Assessment of whether the curve suggests a point-source, propagated, or mixed outbreak pattern. Discussion of the early peak phenomenon.

5. Geographic Distribution (County-Level Analysis) - Incidence rates per 100,000 by county. Identification of the top 5 highest-incidence counties. Urban versus rural disparity analysis. Population density correlation with case rates. County-level mortality rates.

6. Age-Stratified Analysis - Age-specific incidence rates per 100,000. Age-specific hospitalization rates. Age-specific case-fatality rates. Identification of the most affected age groups. Comparison of pediatric versus elderly burden.

7. Strain Characterization and Antiviral Resistance - Subtype distribution across the 12-week period. Documentation of the mid-season strain shift (H1N1 to H3N2 dominance). Week-by-week subtype proportions. Antiviral resistance testing results. Oseltamivir resistance rate and implications.

8. Hospitalization and Mortality Analysis - Weekly hospitalization trends. ICU admission rates and mechanical ventilation rates. Average length of stay analysis. Antiviral treatment rates by county type (urban/suburban/rural). Underlying conditions prevalence among hospitalized patients. Identify and analyze the Harborview County mortality anomaly (significantly elevated deaths during weeks 47-50 suggesting a healthcare system capacity crisis). In-hospital mortality rate comparison across counties.

9. Vaccination Coverage and Effectiveness Correlation - County-level vaccination coverage summary. Age-group specific coverage rates. Year-over-year coverage changes. Statistical correlation between vaccination coverage and case incidence rates, hospitalization rates, and mortality rates. Urban-rural vaccination gap analysis.

10. Healthcare System Impact Assessment - Hospital bed utilization estimates. ICU capacity analysis by county. Primary care provider burden. Healthcare workforce strain indicators. Specific assessment of Harborview County's capacity crisis. Recommendations for surge capacity.

11. Comparison to Prior Seasons - How the 2025-2026 season compares to typical seasons in terms of timing of peak (earlier than usual), severity (case counts, hospitalizations, deaths), dominant strain, and vulnerable populations affected. Assessment of whether this season represents a significant departure.

12. Recommendations - Provide specific, actionable recommendations in three categories. Clinical Recommendations: antiviral treatment protocols, high-risk patient management, testing and diagnosis guidance. Public Health Recommendations: vaccination campaign intensification targets, outbreak control measures, surveillance enhancements, communication strategies. Healthcare System Recommendations: surge capacity activation criteria, mutual aid agreements, resource reallocation priorities, specific interventions for Harborview County.

13. Limitations and Data Quality Notes - Reporting delays and their impact. Underreporting estimates. Testing bias (urban versus rural access). Data completeness assessment. Denominator uncertainties.

14. Statistical Appendix - Key epidemiological rates with 95% confidence intervals where applicable. Summary statistics tables. Rate calculation methodology. County-by-county summary table with all key metrics. Age-group summary table with all key metrics.

Use professional public health report formatting suitable for distribution to county health officers, hospital administrators, and state officials. Include at least 10 tables for quantitative summaries. Bold key findings within narrative paragraphs. Use heading level 1 for the report title, level 2 for section headings, and level 3 for subsections. Include a report date, authors, and distribution list on the title page. Target length is 15 to 20 pages.
